rationale: The dominant near-term regime is unambiguously bearish: nine of 18 FOMC members at the June 16-17 meeting projected at least one rate hike in 2026, core PCE is running at 4.2%, and May NFP printed at double consensus, keeping real yields elevated and the USD bid. The June 30 PCE was firm but non-incremental, suggesting near-term selling exhaustion near $4,000, which tempers conviction; however, June NFP (due July 3) is the next binary catalyst and the base case for another solid print reinforces the hawkish Fed narrative. key_drivers: Fed rate-hike repricing (Sep 2026 hike ~priced, upside risk)
Core PCE at 4.2% — real yields staying elevated
June NFP catalyst (July 3) — consensus still solid
Speculative long unwind from 2025 ATH positioning
Central bank buying collapsed to 16t Q1 2026
US-Iran peace talks diluting safe-haven premium
